overview: Kaleidoscope currently treats every audit, screenshot, and report as an ephemeral one-off. There is no way to track how a project's interface changes over time, detect unintentional regressions, or compare state before and after a code change. This feature introduces a project config, snapshot system, diff engine, baseline manager, HTML diff report, and pre-commit hook integration — giving AI agents and developers a complete visual regression detection workflow.
goals: Enable chronological tracking of interface state across commits
Detect audit regressions (new contrast violations, increased issue counts) automatically
Detect element-level DOM changes (appeared, disappeared, moved, resized) via accessibility tree comparison
Detect pixel-level visual differences between screenshots
Provide structured JSON output for programmatic agent consumption and HTML reports for visual review
Integrate into git pre-commit hooks for automatic regression checks
nonGoals: CI/CD pipeline integration beyond pre-commit hooks
Multi-branch tracking (snapshots are linear, not branch-aware)
Visual regression testing against design mockups (Figma, Sketch)
Automatic remediation (the agent decides; kaleidoscope only reports)
successMetrics: AI agent can detect and report regressions in a pre-commit hook without manual invocation
Diff engine correctly identifies new vs resolved audit issues per category
Element comparison via accessibility tree matches on semantic identity (role + name)
Pixel diff produces visual highlight overlays with configurable similarity threshold
openQuestions: Should snapshot storage deduplicate identical screenshots across runs to save space?
Should `ks diff` support comparing two arbitrary snapshots (not just latest vs baseline)?
rules: All new commands follow the existing `output.Result` JSON convention via `output.Success`/`output.Fail
Snapshot commands reuse existing audit, screenshot, and ax-tree logic internally (no shelling out to the CLI)
Screenshot diffing must be pure Go using standard `image` and `image/png` packages (no ImageMagick)
.ks-project.json` is committed to the repo; `.kaleidoscope/snapshots/` is gitignored
.kaleidoscope/baselines.json` is committed to the repo (shared baseline)
The diff engine is a pure-function module (two data structures in, diff out) with no Chrome dependency

stories.title: Baseline Manager
stories.description:
As an AI agent, I want to accept a snapshot as the new baseline with `ks accept`, so that intentional changes do not trigger false regression alerts in future diff runs.

Introduces `ks accept [snapshot-id]` which promotes a snapshot (default: latest) to baseline for all project URLs by updating `.kaleidoscope/baselines.json`. Supports `--url <path>` to accept for a single URL only.
acceptanceCriteria: ks accept` promotes the latest snapshot to baseline for all project URLs
ks accept <snapshot-id>` promotes a specific snapshot
ks accept --url /dashboard` updates baseline for only that URL path, leaving others unchanged
.kaleidoscope/baselines.json` is correctly updated on disk after accept
ks accept` returns an error if no snapshots exist
Accepting a snapshot that is already the baseline is a no-op (idempotent)
